title: Change all exterior locks
description: Replace or rekey all locks on exterior doors. You don't know how many copies of the old keys are floating around from previous owners, agents, or contractors.

title: Set up a home security system
description: Research and install a home security system with door/window sensors, cameras, and monitoring. Many insurers offer a discount for monitored systems.

title: Test all smoke and carbon monoxide detectors
description: Check every detector in the house, replace batteries, and add detectors to any rooms that are missing them. Place one on every floor and near bedrooms.

title: Set up electricity and gas
description: Transfer or establish electric and gas service in your name. Confirm the switch date so there's no gap in service around your move-in day.

title: Set up internet and cable
description: Research available internet providers at your new address, compare speeds and pricing, and schedule installation. Book early — install appointments can fill up.

title: Set up water and sewer service
description: Contact your municipality to transfer water and sewer into your name. Some areas require an in-person visit or a deposit.

title: Set up trash and recycling collection
description: Determine whether trash pickup is municipal or private in your area. Register for service and find out your collection days and recycling rules.

title: Review and finalize homeowners insurance
description: Make sure your homeowners insurance policy is active and covers replacement cost, not just market value. Consider add-ons for flood, earthquake, or sewer backup if relevant to your area.

title: Get an umbrella insurance policy
description: An umbrella policy provides extra liability coverage beyond your home and auto policies. It's inexpensive relative to the protection it offers, especially as a new homeowner.

title: File for homestead exemption
description: If your state or county offers a homestead exemption, file the paperwork to reduce your property tax bill. There's usually a deadline, so check your local assessor's website promptly.

title: Build a home emergency fund
description: Set aside 1–3% of your home's value annually for unexpected repairs. A busted water heater or roof leak won't wait for your next paycheck.

title: Organize all closing documents
description: Store your closing disclosure, deed, title insurance policy, and mortgage documents in a fireproof safe or secure digital backup. You'll need them for taxes and future refinancing.

title: Understand your mortgage payment schedule
description: Set up your mortgage payment method (auto-pay recommended), confirm the due date, and understand how your payment splits between principal, interest, taxes, and insurance.

title: Locate the main water shut-off valve
description: Find and label the main water shut-off valve so you can act fast in a plumbing emergency. Test it to make sure it turns easily.

title: Locate the electrical panel and label circuits
description: Find your breaker box and label each circuit so you know which breaker controls which room or appliance. This saves major headaches during an outage or repair.

title: Service the HVAC system
description: Schedule an HVAC inspection and tune-up. Replace the air filter and ask about a maintenance plan — regular servicing extends the life of your system significantly.

title: Schedule follow-up on inspection items
description: Review your home inspection report and address any flagged items that weren't resolved before closing. Prioritize safety concerns and anything that could worsen over time.

title: Deep clean or hire a cleaning service
description: Do a thorough deep clean of the entire home before unpacking — it's much easier with empty rooms. Consider a professional service for carpets, ducts, or tough jobs.

title: Update your address everywhere
description: File a change of address with USPS, then update your address with your bank, credit cards, employer, DMV, voter registration, insurance providers, subscriptions, and anywhere else on file.

title: Update your driver's license address
description: Most states require you to update your driver's license within 30 days of moving. Check your state's DMV website for the process — some allow online updates.

title: Register to vote at your new address
description: Update your voter registration with your new address to make sure you're assigned to the correct precinct. Many states let you do this online.

title: Meet your neighbors
description: Introduce yourself to immediate neighbors within the first week or two. They're your first line of local knowledge — and the people most likely to watch out for your home.

title: Learn your local services and emergency contacts
description: Identify the nearest hospital, urgent care, fire station, and police non-emergency number. Save them in your phone so you're not searching in a crisis.

title: Check for HOA rules and community guidelines
description: If your home is in an HOA, read the CC&Rs (covenants, conditions, and restrictions) carefully. Know the rules about landscaping, parking, exterior changes, and dues before you start any projects.
